page: Microsoft Azure M24s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MSMediumMemoryv3 family. Powered by an x86_64 Intel processor, this server provides 24 dedicated virtual CPUs managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 480.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 20.0 GB per vCPU.

The server does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are zero GPUs integrated into this configuration, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data for this specific instance is currently unavailable, limiting direct performance comparisons.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M24s_v3 is designed for workloads requiring high memory density relative to compute resources. The dedicated CPU allocation and large memory capacity make this instance suitable for hosting large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing applications that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure M24s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSMediumMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 480.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high resource density of 20.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing for external storage attachments. With zero GPUs and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is designed for workloads that prioritize memory capacity over local disk speed or graphical acceleration. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the M24s_v3 suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
